repo_prior_art.cite: stat_ate_overlap_decay already uses overlap-tail phase bookkeeping, but for ATE weak-overlap risk rather than a CCOT PRTE endpoint.

published_prior_art.cite: DaouiaFlorensSimar2010FrontierEVT derives boundary-tail normalizations for frontier estimation; it does not cover CCOT PRTE endpoint localization. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/1011.5722.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue Stat entry gives a local experiment for a continuous-instrument CCOT PRTE endpoint.

published_prior_art.cite: TanBlanchetSyrgkanis2026PRTEOT gives continuous-instrument nonparametric rates, and FangSantos2019DirectionalInference gives generic directionally differentiable inference; neither gives this boundary-tail product experiment. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.12263, https://arxiv.org/abs/1404.3763.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo proposal states an endpoint limit law for PRTE OT bounds.

published_prior_art.cite: TanBlanchetSyrgkanis2026PRTEOT reports discrete-instrument asymptotic normality and continuous-instrument rates, while FranguridiLiu2025RegularizedOTPartialID studies entropic regularized OT; neither subsumes the unregularized boundary-local endpoint law. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.12263, https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.18084.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_ate_overlap_decay is the closest in-repo overlap-tail inference-rate neighbor, but it targets ATE weak-overlap minimax risk rather than PRTE OT honest-CI adaptation.

published_prior_art.cite: CaiLow2005AdaptiveCI and RobinsVanderVaart2006AdaptiveSets supply abstract adaptive-CI/confidence-set lower-bound templates; neither embeds the obstruction in a CCOT PRTE model. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0503662, https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0605473.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue PRTE OT endpoint sanity reduction is present.

published_prior_art.cite: ImbensManski2004PartialCI and Stoye2009MorePartialCI are the regular partial-ID endpoint CI template being recovered, so the proposition is mainly a sanity reduction rather than a new frontier.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue entry asks for class-specific honest CIs for the CCOT PRTE boundary endpoint.

published_prior_art.cite: FangSantos2019DirectionalInference and FranguridiLiu2025RegularizedOTPartialID motivate nonsmooth/OT inference, but do not construct fixed-alpha boundary-local CIs for this unregularized endpoint. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/1404.3763, https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.18084.

novelty_flags.label: prop:strict-overlap-reduction / comparator_promise_table
novelty_flags.one_line: The table labels the Imbens-Manski and Stoye comparison as an iff_frontier, but the statement only gives a one-way sanity reduction under strict overlap and does not tighten or characterize their published CI procedures.

soundness_flags.label: thm:local-experiment
soundness_flags.one_line: With T_P(t) comparable to t^alpha and b_n(P) chosen from n b_n^2 V_P(b_n) about one, the raw point measure N_{n,P}(A)=sum 1{B_i/b_n in A} has diverging expected counts for fixed A when alpha<1, so the claimed fixed Poisson boundary factor is not a well-defined limit without an additional centering/rescaling or a different extreme-value radius.

soundness_flags.label: def:endpoint-estimator / thm:endpoint-limit-law
soundness_flags.one_line: hat_tau_n is only described as a sample-split plug-in with localized boundary quantile estimation, while dot_Gamma_P and N_P_alpha are likewise schematic, so the displayed distributional convergence is not yet a well-posed theorem about a specified estimator and limit experiment.

soundness_flags.label: ass:strict-overlap
soundness_flags.one_line: ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML supports propensity overlap for treatment assignment, not the condition B=p(Z,X)-inf_z p(z,X) >= b_so; with B defined as distance to the lower support boundary, this condition is generally empty or nonstandard rather than the cited DML overlap assumption.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:active-face-gap
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The local experiment and endpoint law are conditional on a unique active OT face with a boundary-scaled dual gap, not on resolving tied or slack faces.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The headline limit-law claims are bound to the unique-active-face subclass P_n(alpha).

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:regular-linearization
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The regular interior component is assumed pathwise differentiable away from the boundary-local region.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The phase transition claim concerns the boundary factor plus an assumed regular Gaussian interior component.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:boundary-honest-ci
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: A class-specific CI attaining the oracle rho_n(alpha)^(-1) length is explicitly open.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The no-adaptation lower bound is not claimed to come with an attaining fixed-alpha procedure.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the raw boundary point-measure claim with a precise empirical-process normalization: either use an extreme radius with n T_P(a_n) -> lambda for a Poisson limit, or center/rescale N_{n,P} at b_n(P) and state the resulting Gaussian/stable boundary component.
Write an explicit formula for hat_tau_n, including the localized quantile estimator, trimming/localization rule, sample split, and the plug-in map from eta_P to the CCOT endpoint.

Retag or restate strict overlap as a genuine treatment-propensity/support condition, and downgrade the Imbens-Manski/Stoye comparison from iff_frontier to a sanity reduction unless an actual iff against their regular endpoint assumptions is added.

Add a concrete two-point or modulus construction for thm:no-honest-adaptation, with the two PRTE OT laws, their tail exponents, endpoint separation, and bounded divergence displayed as proposal objects rather than only as a Le Cam handle.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: the novelty is plausibly field-level, but the boundary experiment scaling, estimator definition, and strict-overlap comparator need repair before Stage 0.
